questions.question: To total net sales by area, which fields go where in a PivotTable?
questions.explanation: Categories you list down the side go in Rows; the numbers you summarise go in Values.

questions.question: How do you turn order dates into months and quarters in a pivot?
options: Right-click a date → Group → tick Months/Quarters/Years
questions.explanation: Right-click any date → Group rolls up by Year/Quarter/Month. It needs real dates (Stage 1).

questions.question: Which feature shows each brand's share of total sales as a percentage?
options: Value Field Settings → Show Values As → % of Grand Total
questions.explanation: Show Values As → % of Grand Total converts the sum into a percentage of the whole.

questions.question: How do you make one slicer control several PivotTables at once?
options: Right-click the slicer → Report Connections → tick the pivots
Copy the slicer onto each pivot
questions.explanation: Report Connections links one slicer to multiple pivots — one click filters them all.

questions.question: You want 'net sales by brand manager'. Which pivot Rows field gives it directly?
questions.explanation: Because Stage 2 added BrandManager to every row, you can put it straight on Rows and sum SalesValue.

questions.question: You added new lines to the Sales table but the pivot hasn't changed. What do you do?
options: Right-click the pivot → Refresh (or Data → Refresh All)
questions.explanation: Pivots cache their data — Refresh pulls in the latest. Because Sales is a Table, new rows are included on refresh.
